title: pelargonium torulosum
intro: Pelargonium torulosum, also known as the stately pelargonium, is a species of flowering plant in the family Geraniaceae. It is a shrubby perennial plant with white or pink flowers and is native to South Africa. The leaves and flowers of Pelargonium torulosum have been traditionally used in herbal medicine, exhibiting antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which may be beneficial for soothing digestive issues and reducing fever. Pelargonium torulosum is often prepared as a tea, tincture, or infused oil for its medicinal uses, although its preparations and health benefits should be consulted with a healthcare professional.
